text: Today when I came out to the garden I was checking on my pineapples when I noticed that this one right here is ripe and ready to be picked. So today we're going to harvest it and replant some new ones. Over on this side of the yard I currently have two pineapples. You can see that this one right here is starting to turn yellow but it's not quite ripe yet. But this one right here is nice and golden and ready to be picked. When pineapples are nice and ripe you can pick them by just turning them to the side and they'll pop right off. When pineapples are nice and ripe like this one they have a sweet smell even before you cut into them. It smells incredible. Then what I like to do is go ahead and twist off the top of the pineapple and remove the lower leaves. That way we can replant this in the yard to grow another pineapple. This is the exact same method that I used to grow the pineapple I just harvested by using one of the tops from a pineapple I bought at the grocery store. But when you're growing pineapples you might notice what look like little pineapple tops coming out of the side down beneath the pineapple and these are called pups and you can grow those as well doing the same method. If you keep replanting the pups and the tops before you know it you'll have a whole bunch of pineapples. But the best part about growing your own pineapples is that they're 10 times better than the grocery store because you get to pick them when they're nice and fully ripe rather than them being picked early to sit on the grocery store shelves. And these plants are extremely drought tolerant with very few pests you can quite literally plant them in the yard forget about them and one day you'll walk out and there'll be a pineapple. Till next time get outside and see what you can find.
